title: [Q] systemd in a container: how to get debian:9 up without unconfined?
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind feature

**Description**

<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->
With seccomp at defaults, systemd in a debian:9 container won't startup

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**
[UPDATE] This works on Fedora 29 podman 0.12 so might be a CentOS/RHEL issue

1. create image with systemd-sysv installed from debian:9
buildah run  $CON apt update
buildah run $CON apt install -y systemd systemd-sysv
buildah commit $CON debian_and_systemd:9

2. podman run --entrypoint=/sbin/init --stop-signal=RTMIN+3 debian_and_systemd:9

3.

**Describe the results you received:**
```
Failed to determine whether /sys is a mount point: Operation not permitted
Failed to determine whether /proc is a mount point: Operation not permitted
Failed to determine whether /dev is a mount point: Operation not permitted
Failed to determine whether /dev/shm is a mount point: Operation not permitted
Failed to determine whether /run is a mount point: Operation not permitted
Failed to determine whether /run/lock is a mount point: Operation not permitted
Failed to determine whether /sys/fs/cgroup is a mount point: Operation not permitted
Failed to determine whether /sys/fs/cgroup/systemd is a mount point: Operation not permitted
[!!!!!!] Failed to mount API filesystems, freezing.
Freezing execution.

```
and systemd won't start up in the container


**Describe the results you expected:**
systemd runs in a debian:9 container


**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**
1. debian:9 - works if seccomp is unconfined
1. centos:7 - works
1. ubuntu:18.04 - build images in the same way to install systemd system-sysv; works
1. Host is CentOS 7.6.1810  

I'm trying to understand what is the minimal seccomp to allow the debian:9 systemd to start up.

Is there a recommended way to bisect seccomp between unconfined and default?


**Output of `podman version`:**
```
Version:       0.11.1.1
Go Version:    go1.10.2
OS/Arch:       linux/amd64
```

**Output of `podman info`:**
```
host:
  BuildahVersion: 1.5-dev
  Conmon:
    package: podman-0.11.1.1-3.git594495d.el7.centos.x86_64
    path: /usr/libexec/podman/conmon
    version: 'conmon version 1.12.0-dev, commit: d61f1d176b2b01a5e46efbf236427ece745b92f2-dirty'
  Distribution:
    distribution: '"centos"'
    version: "7"
  MemFree: 96456704
  MemTotal: 2026344448
  OCIRuntime:
    package: runc-1.0.0-57.dev.git2abd837.el7.centos.x86_64
    path: /usr/bin/runc
    version: 'runc version spec: 1.0.0'
  SwapFree: 0
  SwapTotal: 0
  arch: amd64
  cpus: 2
  hostname: localhost.localdomain
  kernel: 3.10.0-957.1.3.el7.x86_64
  os: linux
  rootless: false
  uptime: 917h 12m 30.11s (Approximately 38.21 days)
insecure registries:
  registries: []
registries:
  registries:
  - registry.centos.org
  - registry.access.redhat.com
  - docker.io
  - quay.io
store:
  ContainerStore:
    number: 3
  GraphDriverName: overlay
  GraphOptions:
  - overlay.override_kernel_check=true
  GraphRoot: /var/lib/containers/storage
  GraphStatus:
    Backing Filesystem: xfs
    Native Overlay Diff: "true"
    Supports d_type: "true"
  ImageStore:
    number: 4
  RunRoot: /var/run/containers/storage

```
**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

comments.body: that probably fails because `name_to_handle_at` is not allowed.  Could you try to allow it?
Where is /usr/share/containers/seccomp.json comming from?  We allow a few more rules then the default Docker/Moby version in order to allow systemd to run.

Strangely I don't have this file (recent CentOS 7) containers-common-0.1.31-7.gitb0b750d.el7.centos.x86_64

This may be the reason why debian:9 systemd won't start.
```
/etc/containers
/etc/containers/policy.json
/etc/containers/registries.conf
/etc/containers/registries.d
/etc/containers/registries.d/default.yaml
/etc/containers/storage.conf
/usr/share/containers
/usr/share/containers/mounts.conf
/usr/share/man/man5/containers-storage.conf.5.gz
/usr/share/man/man5/registries.conf.5.gz
/usr/share/rhel/secrets
/usr/share/rhel/secrets/etc-pki-entitlement
/usr/share/rhel/secrets/rhel7.repo
/usr/share/rhel/secrets/rhsm
/var/lib/atomic/sigstore
```

and on Fedora 29 containers-common-0.1.34-1.dev.gite96a9b0.fc29.x86_64

```
/etc/containers
/etc/containers/policy.json
/etc/containers/registries.conf
/etc/containers/registries.d
/etc/containers/registries.d/default.yaml
/etc/containers/storage.conf
/usr/share/containers
/usr/share/containers/mounts.conf
/usr/share/containers/seccomp.json
/usr/share/man/man5/containers-storage.conf.5.gz
/usr/share/man/man5/policy.json.5.gz
/usr/share/man/man5/registries.conf.5.gz
/usr/share/rhel/secrets
/usr/share/rhel/secrets/etc-pki-entitlement
/usr/share/rhel/secrets/rhel7.repo
/usr/share/rhel/secrets/rhsm
/var/lib/atomic/sigstore
```

Fixed by copying the seccomp.json from Fedora 29 containers-common-0.1.34-1.dev.gite96a9b0.fc29.x86_64.

Root cause: packaging issue with CentOS 7 containers-common-0.1.31.

After installing systemd systemd-sysv in debian:9 image

```
# copy seccomp.json from containers-common-0.1.34
podman run -it   --name debian_test --security-opt  seccomp=seccomp.json --entrypoint /sbin/init --stop-signal RTMIN+3 debian_and_systemd:1
```
This works now; thank you @rhatdan  and @giuseppe .
Perhaps we should add a troubleshooting section to comment on this.
